title: mangifera caesia
intro: Mangifera caesia, also known as the black mango or Indian black mango, is a plant species in the family Anacardiaceae, native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Mangifera caesia include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anticancer properties, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
